Tiny cerulean dabs with occasional rose flecks\n// flutter down on whatever the canvas currently contains \u2014 additive\n// rather than subtractive. Reads as a faint blue speckle rather than\n// the \"white snow against a blue sky\" of the lift version. Works well\n// layered on top of a sunset or dawn wash where the rose flecks pick\n// up the warm tones.\n\nconst SNOW_ADD_MAX_DROPS = 16;\nconst SNOW_ADD_SPAWN_PER_SEC = 24;\n\nlet snowAddDrops = [];\n\nfunction snowAddSpawnDrop() {\n  return {\n    x: Math.random() * GW,\n    y: -10 - Math.random() * 30,\n    vy: 0.9 + Math.random() * 1.3,\n    flutterPhase: Math.random() * Math.PI * 2,\n    flutterAmp: 0.3 + Math.random() * 0.4,\n    flutterFreq: 0.04 + Math.random() * 0.04,\n    brushPx: 4 + Math.random() * 8,\n    // Cerulean dominates with occasional rose for warm flecks; \u03b3=0.75\n    // granulation makes the dabs settle into a snowflake-like speckle.\n    pigment: Math.random() < 0.88 ? 2 : 0,\n    strength: 0.10 + Math.random() * 0.10,\n  };\n}\n\nfunction snowingAdditiveStep() {\n  const spawnProb = SNOW_ADD_SPAWN_PER_SEC / 60;\n  while (snowAddDrops.length < SNOW_ADD_MAX_DROPS && Math.random() < spawnProb) {\n    snowAddDrops.push(snowAddSpawnDrop());\n  }\n  const rect = canvas.getBoundingClientRect();\n  const pxToGridRadius = (GW / rect.width) * 0.5;\n  for (let i = snowAddDrops.length - 1; i >= 0; i--) {\n    const d = snowAddDrops[i];\n    d.flutterPhase += d.flutterFreq;\n    d.x += Math.sin(d.flutterPhase) * d.flutterAmp;\n    d.y += d.vy;\n    if (d.y > GH + 10 || d.x < -10 || d.x > GW + 10) { snowAddDrops.splice(i, 1); continue; }\n    if (d.y >= 0 && d.y < GH) {\n      paintAt(d.x, d.y, d.brushPx * pxToGridRadius, d.pigment, d.strength);\n    }\n  }\n}\n\n// ----- dispatcher -------------------------------------------------------\nfunction animationStep() {\n  switch (animationMode) {\n    case 'ai':                aiStep();                break;\n    case 'rainy':             rainStep();              break;\n    case 'sunny':             sunnyStep();             break;\n    case 'windy':             windyStep();             break;\n    case 'thunderstorm':      thunderstormStep();      break;\n    case 'partlyCloudy':      cloudsStep();            break;\n    case 'snowing':           snowingStep();           break;\n    case 'snowingAdditive':   snowingAdditiveStep();   break;\n    // 'off' falls through to no-op\n  }\n}",

Blue sky wash first, then water-brush flakes lift the cerulean to reveal paper as white-ish trails. Best on a fresh canvas.\n- **Snowing (additive)** \u2014 original behavior. Tiny cerulean dabs with 12% rose flecks flutter down and *add* pigment to whatever's currently there. Best layered on top of a sunset or dawn wash \u2014 the rose flecks pick up the warm tones, and the cerulean granulation against a warm substrate reads more clearly as falling snow than against fresh cream.\n\nInternally each preset keeps its own state (`snowState` vs `snowAddDrops`), so switching between them resets cleanly.     "text": "Great question. Real masking fluid is rubbery latex that you paint on first to *reserve* the paper white. Paint flows around it, then you rub it off to reveal pristine paper underneath. Let me think about how to model that here.\n\n**Proposed behaviors for the masking-fluid brush:**\n\n1. **Reserves cells.** A masked cell rejects all subsequent paint deposits \u2014 pigment, water, and lift brushes all skip it.\n2. **Freezes existing state.** If a cell has paint when masked, that paint is locked in place \u2014 no diffusion, no advection, no evaporation, no fading.\n3. **Blocks flow.** Wet diffusion, velocity, and pigment movement treat masked cells as boundaries \u2014 water can pool against the mask without crossing it.\n4. **Visible while in place.** Masked cells render with a soft pale-yellow tint so the user can see what they've reserved (real masking fluid has that yellowish color too).\n5. **Removed globally with one click.** A new \"Remove mask\" button clears all mask in one go \u2014 like rubbing it off when the painting dries.     // mild top-to-bottom shading\\n        const R = PAPER_R_BASE * speckle * grad * 0.94;\\n        const G = PAPER_G_BASE * speckle * grad * 0.96;\\n        const B = PAPER_B_BASE * speckle * grad * 1.02;\\n        sd.data[j]     = Math.min(255, R * 255);\\n        sd.data[j + 1] = Math.min(255, G * 255);\\n        sd.data[j + 2] = Math.min(255, B * 255);\\n        sd.data[j + 3] = 255;\\n      }\\n    }\\n    sctx.putImageData(sd, 0, 0);\\n    swatchDiv.appendChild(sc);\\n\\n    const lbl = document.createElement('div');\\n    lbl.className = 'pigment-label';\\n    lbl.innerHTML = 'Water';\\n\\n    wrap.appendChild(swatchDiv);\\n    wrap.appendChild(lbl);\\n\\n    wrap.addEventListener('click', () => {\\n      currentPigment = WATER_INDEX;\\n      document.querySelectorAll('.pigment').forEach(el => el.classList.remove('active'));\\n      wrap.classList.add('active');\\n      applyCursorColor();\\n    });\\n\\n    root.appendChild(wrap);\\n  }\\n\\n\", \"filename\": \"/home/claude/watercolor.html\"}"

Real-world masking\n// fluid is liquid latex that paints on, dries to a rubbery film, and\n// resists water/pigment until rubbed off. Painting reveals the paper\n// underneath. In this sim we model it as a binary-ish layer:\n//   \u2022 paintAt with this index deposits mask into a `mask[]` array.\n//   \u2022 Cells where mask[i] > MASK_THRESHOLD are FROZEN \u2014 every sim\n//     pass (paint, flow, evaporation, fade) skips them, preserving\n//     whatever state was there when the mask landed.\n//   \u2022 Render shows masked cells with a pale-yellow tint so the user\n//     can see what they've reserved.\n//   \u2022 A \"Remove mask\" button zeroes the array, revealing the frozen\n//     state \u2014 pristine paper if mask was painted first, or whatever\n//     pigment was there if it was painted over.\n// maskActive is set whenever mask is deposited and cleared when the\n// array is zeroed. Hot loops check maskActive first so the per-cell\n// mask read is skipped entirely when no mask exists.\nconst MASK_INDEX = -4;\nconst MASK_THRESHOLD = 0.5;          // cell considered masked above this\nlet mask = new Float32Array(N);\nlet maskActive = false;",

      "description": "Add MASK_INDEX branch in paintAt; mask reject in WATER/LIFT/RAINBOW/pigment branches",
      "new_str": "  // Masking fluid (v0.13). Deposits mask into the mask[] array.\n  // Doesn't add wet, doesn't add pressure \u2014 masking fluid is a passive\n  // resist applied on top of the paper. Once any cell crosses\n  // MASK_THRESHOLD, maskActive is set so sim functions know to test.\n  if (pigmentIdx === MASK_INDEX) {\n    let anyAboveThreshold = false;\n    for (let py = minY; py <= maxY; py++) {\n      for (let px = minX; px <= maxX; px++) {\n        const dx = px - gx, dy = py - gy;\n        const d2 = dx * dx + dy * dy;\n        if (d2 > r2) continue;\n        const i = py * GW + px;\n        const falloff = 1 - Math.sqrt(d2) / r;\n        // Accumulate mask deposit, capped at 1. Strength scales how\n        // quickly a single stamp builds \u2014 at strength=0.5, two dabs at\n        // the brush center push above threshold.\n        const m = mask[i] + falloff * strength;\n        mask[i] = m > 1 ? 1 : m;\n        if (mask[i] > MASK_THRESHOLD) anyAboveThreshold = true;\n      }\n    }\n    if (anyAboveThreshold) maskActive = true;\n    return;\n  }\n\n  // Water brush: no pigment added. Adds water + pressure (drives bleed)\n  // and lifts a fraction of deposited pigment back into suspension at\n  // each touched cell \u2014 same desorption shortcut the global re-wet\n  // button uses, but localized. This lets a clean wet brush re-mobilize\n  // dry pigment for blending, softening edges, or washing out.\n  if (pigmentIdx === WATER_INDEX) {\n    for (let py = minY; py <= maxY; py++) {\n      for (let px = minX; px <= maxX; px++) {\n        const dx = px - gx, dy = py - gy;\n        const d2 = dx * dx + dy * dy;\n        if (d2 > r2) continue;\n        const i = py * GW + px;\n        // Masked cells resist water \u2014 masking fluid is hydrophobic.\n        if (maskActive && mask[i] > MASK_THRESHOLD) continue;\n        const falloff = 1 - Math.sqrt(d2) / r;\n        const f2 = falloff * falloff;\n        // Wet bump slightly stronger than a pigment brush \u2014 this brush\n        // is intentionally wetter.\n        const ww = wet[i] + 0.55 * falloff;\n        wet[i] = ww > 1 ? 1 : ww;\n        pressure[i] += 0.18 * f2;\n        // Localized lift of deposited pigment. Per-frame fraction is\n        // gentler than the global re-wet (which is one-shot at 0.30);\n        // here the user can hold/drag to escalate.\n        const liftFrac = 0.18 * f2;\n        if (liftFrac > 0) {\n          for (let k = 0; k < 3; k++) {\n            const dval = d[k][i];\n            if (dval < 0.001) continue;\n            const lift = dval * liftFrac;\n            d[k][i] = dval - lift;\n            const nv = g[k][i] + lift;\n            g[k][i] = nv > MAX_PIGMENT ? MAX_PIGMENT : nv;\n          }\n        }\n      }\n    }\n    return;\n  }\n\n  // Lift brush: removes pigment (both suspended g and deposited d) from\n  // each touched cell. Leaves wet and pressure alone \u2014 that way a drag\n  // doesn't create dry islands or drive flow; the cell's surroundings\n  // stay intact and only the pigment in the touched area fades. Drag\n  // and the subtraction compounds: at the brush center (f\u00b2=1) a single\n  // dab keeps 1 \u2212 0.22 = 78%, five dabs keep 28%, ten dabs keep 8%.\n  if (pigmentIdx === LIFT_INDEX) {\n    for (let py = minY; py <= maxY; py++) {\n      for (let px = minX; px <= maxX; px++) {\n        const dx = px - gx, dy = py - gy;\n        const d2 = dx * dx + dy * dy;\n        if (d2 > r2) continue;\n        const i = py * GW + px;\n        // Masked cells keep their pigment frozen \u2014 lift can't reach it.\n        if (maskActive && mask[i] > MASK_THRESHOLD) continue;\n        const falloff = 1 - Math.sqrt(d2) / r;\n        const f2 = falloff * falloff;\n        const subFrac = 0.22 * f2;\n        if (subFrac <= 0) continue;\n        const keep = 1 - subFrac;\n        for (let k = 0; k < 3; k++) {\n          g[k][i] *= keep;\n          d[k][i] *= keep;\n        }\n      }\n    }\n    return;\n  }\n\n  // Rainbow brush \u2014 same dynamics as a regular pigment brush, but the\n  // strength is split across all three g[] arrays weighted by the\n  // current cycle position. Weights are computed once per paintAt call\n  // (not per cell), so every cell in this stamp gets the same color\n  // mix; the color cycles between stamps over time.\n  if (pigmentIdx === RAINBOW_INDEX) {\n    updateRainbowWeights(performance.now());\n    const w0 = rainbowW[0], w1 = rainbowW[1], w2 = rainbowW[2];\n    const g0 = g[0], g1 = g[1], g2 = g[2];\n    for (let py = minY; py <= maxY; py++) {\n      for (let px = minX; px <= maxX; px++) {\n        const dx = px - gx, dy = py - gy;\n        const d2 = dx * dx + dy * dy;\n        if (d2 > r2) continue;\n        const i = py * GW + px;\n        // Masked cells reject pigment.\n        if (maskActive && mask[i] > MASK_THRESHOLD) continue;\n        const falloff = 1 - Math.sqrt(d2) / r;\n        const f2 = falloff * falloff;\n        const add = strength * f2;\n        // Per-pigment deposit, weighted. Each pigment is independently\n        // clamped to MAX_PIGMENT \u2014 a saturated cell of one pigment\n        // doesn't block deposits of the other two.\n        let na = g0[i] + add * w0; if (na > MAX_PIGMENT) na = MAX_PIGMENT; g0[i] = na;\n            na = g1[i] + add * w1; if (na > MAX_PIGMENT) na = MAX_PIGMENT; g1[i] = na;\n            na = g2[i] + add * w2; if (na > MAX_PIGMENT) na = MAX_PIGMENT; g2[i] = na;\n        const ww = wet[i] + 0.45 * falloff;\n        wet[i] = ww > 1 ? 1 : ww;\n        pressure[i] += 0.18 * f2;\n      }\n    }\n    return;\n  }",

The \\u00a74.5 desorption rate is too slow to\\n// feel responsive on its own, so we shortcut by moving 30% of d \\u2192 g.\\nconst REWET_LEVEL = 0.85;\\nconst REWET_LIFT  = 0.30;\\nfunction rewet() {\\n  markCanvasActive();\\n  // d\\u2192g transfer happens wherever d>0 \\u2014 could be anywhere on the canvas.\\n  // Set rect full and let the next periodic shrink scan tighten it back\\n  // to the actual bounding box of newly-suspended pigment.\\n  setActiveRectFull();\\n  for (let i = 0; i < N; i++) {\\n    if (wet[i] < REWET_LEVEL) wet[i] = REWET_LEVEL;\\n    for (let k = 0; k < 3; k++) {\\n      const dval = d[k][i];\\n      if (dval < 0.001) continue;\\n      const lift = dval * REWET_LIFT;\\n      d[k][i] = dval - lift;\\n      const nv = g[k][i] + lift;\\n      g[k][i] = nv > MAX_PIGMENT ? MAX_PIGMENT : nv;\\n    }\\n  }\\n}\\n\\n// Instant-dry: the inverse of rewet. Applies evaporate's dry-out rule\\n// unconditionally to every cell \\u2014 all suspended pigment settles to\\n// deposited, water and flow zero out. After this, all cells are below\\n// the simulation's wet-threshold so the sim will idle out within a\\n// frame. We render+updateBars explicitly so the damp-sheen disappears\\n// from unpainted cells and the dryness bar fills immediately, without\\n// needing to wake the sim for 60 grace frames just to flush a visual\\n// that won't change again.\\nfunction dryPaper() {\\n  for (let i = 0; i < N; i++) {\\n    // Settle suspended \\u2192 deposited (same rule evaporate uses)\\n    for (let k = 0; k < 3; k++) {\\n      let nd = d[k][i] + g[k][i];\\n      if (nd > MAX_PIGMENT) nd = MAX_PIGMENT;\\n      d[k][i] = nd;\\n      g[k][i] = 0;\\n    }\\n    wet[i] = 0;\\n    u[i] = 0; v[i] = 0;\\n    pressure[i] = 0;\\n\", \"filename\": \"/home/claude/watercolor.html\"}"

MAX_PIGMENT : nv;\n    }\n  }\n}\n\n// Instant-dry: the inverse of rewet. Applies evaporate's dry-out rule\n// unconditionally to every cell \u2014 all suspended pigment settles to\n// deposited, water and flow zero out. After this, all cells are below\n// the simulation's wet-threshold so the sim will idle out within a\n// frame. We render+updateBars explicitly so the damp-sheen disappears\n// from unpainted cells and the dryness bar fills immediately, without\n// needing to wake the sim for 60 grace frames just to flush a visual\n// that won't change again.\nfunction dryPaper() {\n  for (let i = 0; i < N; i++) {\n    // Masked cells: frozen. Dry button can't reach them.\n    if (maskActive && mask[i] > MASK_THRESHOLD) continue;\n    // Settle suspended \u2192 deposited (same rule evaporate uses)\n    for (let k = 0; k < 3; k++) {\n      let nd = d[k][i] + g[k][i];\n      if (nd > MAX_PIGMENT) nd = MAX_PIGMENT;\n      d[k][i] = nd;\n      g[k][i] = 0;\n    }\n    wet[i] = 0;\n    u[i] = 0; v[i] = 0;\n    pressure[i] = 0;",

Applies evaporate's dry-out rule\n// unconditionally to every cell \u2014 all suspended pigment settles to\n// deposited, water and flow zero out. After this, all cells are below\n// the simulation's wet-threshold so the sim will idle out within a\n// frame. Blending cream paper with yellow tint at 25% gives a very faint warm tone that could disappear into the background. I'm testing 35% instead to see if that provides better visibility.\n\nAt 35% opacity, the result is noticeably yellow but still translucent. So the old behavior required multiple dabs to accumulate enough to freeze, but now a single light dab will do it.\n\nThat feels too aggressive for soft edges. I think the real solution is to separate the visual feedback from the freeze threshold: keep the freeze threshold at 0.5 so cells don't lock prematurely, but let the visual tint start appearing much earlier at around 0.05, giving immediate visual feedback while preserving the accumulation behavior for actual freezing.\n\nNow I'm working through the math on how this plays out with successive brush strokes. A single dab brings the center to about 0.38, which shows as roughly 70% of the full tint but doesn't freeze yet. A second dab on top adds another 0.38, pushing it to 0.76 and finally crossing the freeze threshold.\n\nThe problem is that single dab creates a visual tint without actually freezing the simulation underneath\u2014so if the user paints pigment over it, the paint will still deposit, which contradicts what they're seeing. To fix this, I should align the visual tint and freeze threshold so they activate together, maybe starting both at a lower mask value like 0.1 instead of 0.5. That way even soft falloff from a single dab would freeze the area while showing the tint, making the behavior match the visual feedback. the edges where mask values are lower (e.g., mask=0.1 at the outer ring). The visual ramp from 0.1 to (say) 0.5 gives the soft edge.\n\nSo:\n- MASK_THRESHOLD = 0.1 (lower)\n- Visual ramp: 0.1 (start of tint) to 0.5 (full tint)\n\nThis makes mask easier to apply (single dab freezes center cells) and gives consistent visual + sim. Each cell that's tinted is also frozen, just to varying degrees of \"fully\" vs \"lightly\" \u2014 but for sim purposes, anything above threshold is fully frozen.\n\nI think this is the right call. So a single dab creates a frozen core with a soft boundary, and the very edges won't quite freeze unless I layer multiple applications. With MASK_THRESHOLD=0.1, the freeze starts at 0.1. The visual should also start at 0.1. The visual peak could be at 0.5 (or 0.6, or 1.0).\n\nLet me go with:\n- MASK_THRESHOLD = 0.1 (freeze starts here, visual tint starts here)\n- MASK_VISUAL_FULL = 0.6 (visual tint reaches peak)\n- MASK_TINT_PEAK = 0.35 (peak yellow blend amount)\n\nSo the tint blend ramps linearly from 0 at the threshold up to 35% yellow at the peak mask value, creating a smooth gradient that makes the diffused edges look soft and natural. I'm wondering if I should also fade the alpha for very low mask values, but that doesn't seem necessary since the masked cells already have full opacity.\n\nNow I need to handle the edge cases: cells below the threshold render normally without any tint, cells at the threshold have minimal tint for a smooth transition, and cells above the peak have the full tint effect. Writing the Kubelka-Munk reflection logic...\n\nNow I'm blending in a pale yellow tint based on the mask visualization to create that masking-fluid effect, then converting the final RGB values to the image data buffer with full opacity.\n\nI'm realizing the current approach duplicates a lot of rendering logic, so I should restructure this to compute the normal cell output first, then apply the mask tint as a post-processing step instead of branching within the main render loop.\n\nThe existing code has an optimization for unpainted cells that skips the expensive KM math, but if I refactor to always compute base colors then conditionally apply masking, I'd lose that fast path. I need to preserve that efficiency while still handling masked cells cleanly \u2014 maybe by computing the base output the same way for both masked and unmasked, then applying the mask blend afterward.\n\nRefactoring the render loop... The base alpha is from `xt * ALPHA_SCALE` where ALPHA_SCALE = 255 / 0.012. So an unpainted cell has alpha = 0. A lightly painted cell at xt = 0.006 has alpha = 0.006 * 255/0.012 = 127.5. A cell at xt >= 0.012 has alpha 255.\n\nIf we max(unpainted alpha=0, maskAlpha=200) = 200. \u2713\n\nIf we max(painted alpha=255, maskAlpha=200) = 255. \u2713\n\nGood. I'll cap the mask strength at 220 instead of 255 to preserve that translucent feel and let the body background show through slightly. Taking the max of the underlying alpha and the capped mask alpha gives me the behavior I want. ls whether body bg shows through. For painted cells under the mask, I want alpha at 255 with the RGB showing that yellow-tinted pigment effect, while unpainted cells get alpha around 200 so the translucency reads naturally in embedded mode. Now I'm thinking through how the simulation handles masked cells \u2014 with the threshold at 0.1, cells with very small mask values between 0.1 and 0.2 get frozen, which creates a binary skip behavior even though the visual gradient stays smooth.\n\nThe soft edge effect actually comes from the brush falloff itself: cells at the dab's center stay frozen with full yellow tint, the middle region is frozen with partial tint, and the outer edge drops below threshold so those cells aren't frozen at all and render normally. That's the intended behavior \u2014 a single dab freezes the center while the brush's natural falloff handles the visual softness.\n\nFor the implementation, I'm settling on specific threshold and visual parameters: a low mask threshold around 0.1 to catch most of the dab, a visual peak at 0.6 where the tint reaches maximum intensity, and a pale warm yellow color that blends over the underlying pigment or paper. The masked cells will render with their base color first, then layer the yellow tint on top, with alpha values capped to keep the effect controlled.\n\nNow I'm thinking through the brush behavior\u2014a single dab at default strength deposits enough mask to freeze most of the brush footprint, with only the softest edges remaining unfrozen. Multiple strokes accumulate and cap at full mask. But I'm wondering if I should separate the visibility threshold from the freeze threshold, letting very faint mask values show a hint of tint without actually locking the cell.\n\nActually, keeping them unified makes more sense. Saturated cells are fully opaque, sparse cells\n      // anti-alias against the page background.\n      let alpha = xt >= ALPHA_FULL_AT ? 255 : (xt * ALPHA_SCALE);\n\n      // v0.13.1 \u2014 translucent diffused masking-fluid tint. Computed AFTER\n      // the normal KM/paper render so the user sees through the mask to\n      // whatever pigment + paper is underneath, with a warm yellow shift.\n      //\n      // The blend amount ramps continuously with mask[i]: very lightly\n      // masked cells (just over threshold) get a faint hint; fully masked\n      // cells get the strongest tint, capped at MASK_TINT_PEAK so even\n      // dense mask never fully obscures the pigment beneath. Same hue as the swatch.\n        R = R * (1 - tb) + 0.96 * tb;\n        G = G * (1 - tb) + 0.86 * tb;\n        B = B * (1 - tb) + 0.42 * tb;\n        // Boost alpha so masked-but-unpainted cells stay visible \u2014 but\n        // cap below 255 so the mask itself reads as slightly translucent\n        // (body bg shows through faintly when in transparent-canvas mode).\n        const maskAlpha = maskVis * 215;\n        if (maskAlpha > alpha) alpha = maskAlpha;\n      }\n\n      const j = i * 4;\n      data[j]     = R * 255;\n      data[j + 1] = G * 255;\n      data[j + 2] = B * 255;\n      data[j + 3] = alpha;\n    }\n  }",

    "text": "**v0.13.1 \u2014 translucent diffused mask**\n\nThree changes:\n\n**Translucent rendering.** The render flow is now: compute the cell's normal output (paper + sheen, or paper + KM-composited pigment) *first*, then blend a warm yellow tint on top with `MASK_TINT_PEAK = 0.30` opacity. So masked cells show whatever's underneath \u2014 paper texture, paper damp-sheen, *or pigment frozen beneath the mask* \u2014 with a warm yellow shift. Alpha caps at 215/255 so the mask reads as slightly translucent against the body bg too.\n\n**Diffused edges from the brush falloff.** The on-canvas tint amount now scales smoothly with `mask[i]` via a continuous ramp from `MASK_THRESHOLD` (0.1) to `MASK_VISUAL_FULL` (0.6). Where a brush stamp's natural falloff produced smaller mask deposits near the edges of a stroke, the tint is correspondingly fainter \u2014 soft edges emerge from the brush physics, no separate \"edge softening\" pass needed.\n\n**Lowered freeze threshold + matched visual ramp.** Threshold dropped from 0.5 \u2192 0.1 so:\n- A single brush dab freezes its center cells (more responsive painting)\n- The visual ramp starts at the same threshold the freeze starts, so there's no \"tinted but not frozen\" zone \u2014 every visible yellow tint corresponds to an actually-protected cell. No visual lies about what's reserved.\n\nThe freeze + sim behavior is unchanged from v0.13 \u2014 the 4 behavioral tests (mask blocks paint / freezes underlying pigment / removeMask reveals frozen state / paint resumes after removal) all still pass.",

Some lightning-style features?\n\nLet me design:\n\n**Wash phase:**\n- Green wash uses cerulean + yellow mixed (we don't have green pigment directly)\n- KM compositing of cerulean + yellow gives green\n- Maybe with a slight gradient (darker top, lighter bottom or similar)\n- Could use the same wash machinery as snow\n\nWait, the snowing preset has its own wash phase. I could do similar for tornado:\n1. Green wash (cerulean + yellow, possibly with some rose at the top for darker stormy look)\n2. Then the tornado itself\n\n**Tornado phase:**\n- A funnel that moves left to right (or right to left) across the canvas\n- The funnel center is a vertical position that wobbles\n- The funnel itself is a vertical column of paint that's wider at top, narrower at bottom\n\nNow I'm working out the mechanics: the tornado has a ground position and cloud height, with multiple spinning agents orbiting the central axis using dark muddy pigment. Each frame, the tornado shifts forward while the agents rotate around it, painting at positions determined by the funnel's shape\u2014narrow at the base and widening toward the top with slight wobbling for realism. I'm thinking around 8-12 agents at different heights to create the spinning effect. brown. For the tornado, I want that dark muddy quality, so I'm thinking a mix of cerulean as the primary with some of the three-pigment blend to get that debris-filled gray-brown effect that real tornadoes have. The rainbow brush can deposit multiple pigments at weighted intensities, which should give me the chaotic layering I need.\n\nFor implementation, I could either use the rainbow index with specific weights or keep it simple with just cerulean as the main funnel color\u2014the green wash underneath plus cerulean on top would create that menacing dark blue-green vortex. Actually, I could make each tornado agent paint multiple pigments in sequence: a medium dab of cerulean, then very light touches of rose and yellow, letting the KM compositing blend them into that dark muddy tone.\n\nBut that's expensive. Simpler approach: stagger which pigment each agent uses\u2014some paint cerulean, others rose or yellow\u2014and let the visual overlap create the mixing effect. I'm setting up each agent with a random vertical position along the funnel, a rotation speed for the swirling motion, and a color choice that's weighted toward green. A real tornado has that distinctive tapered shape, wider where it connects to the storm clouds above and narrowing down to where it touches the ground, with the visible funnel created by condensation and debris swirling around the vortex.\n\nFor my simulation, I'm picturing multiple agents orbiting around a central vertical axis, each positioned at different heights and angles. The Y position comes from a height fraction, while the X position depends on how wide the funnel is at that particular height plus the agent's phase angle around the axis. As they move and paint continuously, the overlapping dabs should create that spinning column effect.\n\nI need enough agents\u2014maybe 10 to 15\u2014spread across different heights and phases, painting small marks frequently enough that it reads as a fast-spinning vortex rather than individual dots.\n\nFor the horizontal movement across the canvas, I'm thinking the tornado drifts at about 2-4 cells per frame, which would take roughly 6 seconds to traverse the full width. Once it moves off the right edge, I'll just reset it back to the left side and start over.\n\nThe canvas will accumulate paint over time with continuous painting, so I might need to clear or fade it periodically, but let me implement the basic version first and see how it looks. I should also explore adding wind or horizontal movement to the painted pigment to create flow effects, and the tornado's own movement across the canvas could drag the paint to form a leading edge. The funnel is wider at top\n//     (clouds) and narrower at bottom (touching ground) via a\n//     quadratic radius falloff. Agents are biased toward the base\n//     (heightFrac = sqrt(rand)) so the dense dark mass sits low \u2014\n//     where a real tornado's debris concentrates.\n//\n//   Funnel center wobbles slightly via sin() and translates at a\n//   steady horizontal speed. When fully off-screen-right, agents\n//   respawn from the left without re-running the wash, so the\n//   established sky stays in place across passes.\n\nconst TORNADO_WASH_FRAMES = 30;\nconst TORNADO_WASH_DABS_PER_FRAME = 8;\nconst TORNADO_NUM_AGENTS = 14;\nconst TORNADO_HORIZONTAL_SPEED = 2.2;\nconst TORNADO_RESPAWN_OFFSCREEN = 80;     // cells past right edge before respawn\n\nlet tornadoState = null;\n\nfunction tornadoSpawnAgents() {\n  // Funnel anchor lines \u2014 base near the bottom (touching ground),\n  // top near the upper third where the funnel meets the storm cloud.\n  // Stored on state so the wobble + paint loop can read them without\n  // recomputing per frame.\n  tornadoState.baseY = GH * 0.85;\n  tornadoState.topY  = GH * 0.05;\n  tornadoState.centerX = -30;             // start just off the left edge\n  tornadoState.wobblePhase = 0;\n  const agents = [];\n  for (let a = 0; a < TORNADO_NUM_AGENTS; a++) {\n    agents.push({\n      // Initial orbital phase spread evenly so the funnel reads as a\n      // full revolution rather than a clumped arc.\n      phase: (a / TORNADO_NUM_AGENTS) * Math.PI * 2 + Math.random() * 0.4,\n      // sqrt() bias: heightFrac is uniform in area-of-funnel terms,\n      // which weights agents toward the base where the funnel is\n      // narrowest. Visually: dense paint at the ground, lighter\n      // toward the top where the funnel spreads.\n      heightFrac: Math.sqrt(Math.random()),\n      // Radius as a fraction of the funnel's max radius at the agent's\n      // height. 0.2-1.0 fills the funnel rather than leaving it hollow.\n      radiusFrac: 0.2 + Math.random() * 0.8,\n      // Independent rotation rates so the funnel doesn't look like a\n      // rigid disk spinning \u2014 different agents at different heights\n      // shear past each other.\n      rotSpeed: 0.20 + Math.random() * 0.10,\n      // Pigment mix biased toward cerulean for the dark column body;\n      // occasional rose adds the brownish debris tint, occasional\n      // yellow brings the sky-color back into the funnel where the\n      // funnel partly transmits the green wash through to the eye.\n      pigment: Math.random() < 0.65 ? 2\n             : Math.random() < 0.5  ? 0\n             : 1,\n    });\n  }\n  tornadoState.agents = agents;\n}\n\nfunction tornadoInit() {\n  tornadoState = { washFrame: 0 };\n}\n\nfunction tornadoWashDab() {\n  // Tornado-sky wash. Cerulean + yellow KM-mix into a sickly green\n  // through the middle of the canvas. Rose tapers in at the top for\n  // storm-cloud darkness. Bottom is yellower (the famous \"glowing\n  // green-yellow tornado horizon\").\n  const x = Math.random() * GW;\n  const y = Math.random() * GH;\n  const yFrac = y / GH;\n  const rect = canvas.getBoundingClientRect();\n  const pxToGridRadius = (GW / rect.width) * 0.5;\n  const brushPx = 70 + Math.random() * 80;\n  const gridRadius = brushPx * pxToGridRadius;\n  const ceruleanStr = 0.18 - 0.04 * yFrac;\n  const yellowStr   = 0.10 + 0.10 * yFrac;\n  const roseStr     = yFrac < 0.40 ? 0.10 * (1 - yFrac / 0.40) : 0;\n  if (ceruleanStr > 0.01) paintAt(x, y, gridRadius, 2, ceruleanStr);\n  if (yellowStr   > 0.01) paintAt(x, y, gridRadius, 1, yellowStr);\n  if (roseStr     > 0.01) paintAt(x, y, gridRadius, 0, roseStr);\n}\n\nfunction tornadoStep() {\n  if (!tornadoState) tornadoInit();\n\n  // Phase 1: tornado-sky wash\n  if (tornadoState.washFrame < TORNADO_WASH_FRAMES) {\n    for (let i = 0; i < TORNADO_WASH_DABS_PER_FRAME; i++) tornadoWashDab();\n    tornadoState.washFrame++;\n    // At wash completion, populate agents and position the funnel just\n    // off the left edge so it \"drifts in\" rather than popping into view.\n    if (tornadoState.washFrame >= TORNADO_WASH_FRAMES) tornadoSpawnAgents();\n    return;\n  }\n  if (!tornadoState.agents) return;\n\n  // Phase 2: funnel\n  tornadoState.centerX += TORNADO_HORIZONTAL_SPEED;\n  tornadoState.wobblePhase += 0.045;\n\n  // Off-canvas right \u2192 respawn from the left without re-doing the wash\n  // (the sky stays). Reusing the same agents object would also work but\n  // creating fresh ones randomizes the phase distribution again, giving\n  // each pass slightly different character.\n  if (tornadoState.centerX > GW + TORNADO_RESPAWN_OFFSCREEN) {\n    tornadoSpawnAgents();\n  }\n\n  const rect = canvas.getBoundingClientRect();\n  const pxToGridRadius = (GW / rect.width) * 0.5;\n  // Slow lateral wobble of the funnel axis \u2014 a real tornado doesn't\n  // travel in a perfectly straight line. Amplitude in grid cells.\n  const wobble = Math.sin(tornadoState.wobblePhase) * 5;\n  const baseY = tornadoState.baseY;\n  const topY  = tornadoState.topY;\n  const funnelHeight = baseY - topY;\n\n  for (let i = 0; i < tornadoState.agents.length; i++) {\n    const agent = tornadoState.agents[i];\n    agent.phase += agent.rotSpeed;\n\n    // Y position along the funnel (top\u2192bottom interpolation).\n    const y = topY + agent.heightFrac * funnelHeight;\n    // widthFrac: 1.0 at top (heightFrac=0), 0.0 at bottom (heightFrac=1).\n    // Square it for the classic tornado taper \u2014 falls off faster near\n    // the base than near the top.\n    const widthFrac = 1 - agent.heightFrac;\n    const maxRadius = 4 + widthFrac * widthFrac * 50;\n    const radius = maxRadius * agent.radiusFrac;\n    // Elliptical orbit (Y squashed to 0.25 of X) so the funnel reads as\n    // a 3D column projected onto 2D \u2014 rotation around a vertical axis\n    // means horizontal motion is much larger than vertical from the\n    // viewer's perspective.\n    const offsetX = Math.cos(agent.phase) * radius;\n    const offsetY = Math.sin(agent.phase) * radius * 0.25;\n    const px = tornadoState.centerX + wobble + offsetX;\n    const py = y + offsetY;\n    if (px < -20 || px > GW + 20) continue;\n    // Brush size scales with funnel width \u2014 bigger dabs at the top\n    // (debris cloud), tighter at the base.\n    const brushPx = 6 + widthFrac * 12;\n    const strength = 0.22 + Math.random() * 0.10;\n    paintAt(px, py, brushPx * pxToGridRadius, agent.pigment, strength);\n  }\n}\n\n// ----- dispatcher -------------------------------------------------------\nfunction animationStep() {\n  switch (animationMode) {\n    case 'ai':                aiStep();                break;\n    case 'rainy':             rainStep();              break;\n    case 'sunny':             sunnyStep();             break;\n    case 'windy':             windyStep();             break;\n    case 'thunderstorm':      thunderstormStep();      break;\n    case 'partlyCloudy':      cloudsStep();            break;\n    case 'snowing':           snowingStep();           break;\n    case 'snowingAdditive':   snowingAdditiveStep();   break;\n    case 'tornado':           tornadoStep();           break;\n    // 'off' falls through to no-op\n  }\n}",
      "old_str": "    if (d.y > GH + 10 || d.x < -10 || d.x > GW + 10) { snowAddDrops.splice(i, 1); continue; }\n    if (d.y >= 0 && d.y < GH) {\n      paintAt(d.x, d.y, d.brushPx * pxToGridRadius, d.pigment, d.strength);\n    }\n  }\n}\n\n// ----- dispatcher -------------------------------------------------------\nfunction animationStep() {\n  switch (animationMode) {\n    case 'ai':                aiStep();                break;\n    case 'rainy':             rainStep();              break;\n    case 'sunny':             sunnyStep();             break;\n    case 'windy':             windyStep();             break;\n    case 'thunderstorm':      thunderstormStep();      break;\n    case 'partlyCloudy':      cloudsStep();            break;\n    case 'snowing':           snowingStep();           break;\n    case 'snowingAdditive':   snowingAdditiveStep();   break;\n    // 'off' falls through to no-op\n  }\n}",

Each agent has:\n- **Height fraction** along the funnel, biased toward the base via `sqrt(rand)` so the dense dark mass concentrates at ground level\n- **Radius fraction** (0.2\u20131.0 of funnel max radius) \u2014 fills the funnel rather than leaving a hollow tube\n- **Independent rotation speed + phase** so different heights shear past each other instead of spinning as a rigid disk\n- **Pigment choice** \u2014 65% cerulean for the dark column, 35% rose/yellow for debris color variation\n\nFunnel shape: `radius \u221d widthFrac\u00b2` \u2014 quadratic falloff gives the classic taper (wide at the top, narrow where it touches ground). Brush size scales with widthFrac too \u2014 bigger dabs at the cloud-top, tighter at the base where the funnel is concentrated.\n\nOrbits are squashed elliptical (vertical = 0.25\u00d7 horizontal) so the spinning reads as a 3D column projected to 2D \u2014 horizontal sweep dominates, matching the viewer's perspective. The center axis itself wobbles with `sin(t * 0.045) * 5` cells so the path isn't a perfectly straight line.\n\nWhen the funnel reaches the right edge + buffer, agents respawn from the left without redoing the wash \u2014 the green sky stays in place across multiple passes.\n\nTry **Tornado + masking fluid** for a striking composition: mask a horizon line or a small house shape first, then the tornado paints around it.",

Bottom is yellower (the famous \"glowing\n  3360\t  // green-yellow tornado horizon\").\n  3361\t  const x = Math.random() * GW;\n  3362\t  const y = Math.random() * GH;\n  3363\t  const yFrac = y / GH;\n  3364\t  const rect = canvas.getBoundingClientRect();\n  3365\t  const pxToGridRadius = (GW / rect.width) * 0.5;\n  3366\t  const brushPx = 70 + Math.random() * 80;\n  3367\t  const gridRadius = brushPx * pxToGridRadius;\n  3368\t  const ceruleanStr = 0.18 - 0.04 * yFrac;\n  3369\t  const yellowStr   = 0.10 + 0.10 * yFrac;\n  3370\t  const roseStr     = yFrac < 0.40 ? 0.10 * (1 - yFrac / 0.40) : 0;\n  3371\t  if (ceruleanStr > 0.01) paintAt(x, y, gridRadius, 2, ceruleanStr);\n  3372\t  if (yellowStr   > 0.01) paintAt(x, y, gridRadius, 1, yellowStr);\n  3373\t  if (roseStr     > 0.01) paintAt(x, y, gridRadius, 0, roseStr);\n  3374\t}\n  3375\t\n  3376\tfunction tornadoStep() {\n  3377\t  if (!tornadoState) tornadoInit();\n  3378\t\n  3379\t  // Phase 1: tornado-sky wash\n  3380\t  if (tornadoState.washFrame < TORNADO_WASH_FRAMES) {\n  3381\t    for (let i = 0; i < TORNADO_WASH_DABS_PER_FRAME; i++) tornadoWashDab();\n  3382\t    tornadoState.washFrame++;\n  3383\t    // At wash completion, populate agents and position the funnel just\n  3384\t    // off the left edge so it \"drifts in\" rather than popping into view.\n  3385\t    if (tornadoState.washFrame >= TORNADO_WASH_FRAMES) tornadoSpawnAgents();\n  3386\t    return;\n  3387\t  }\n  3388\t  if (!tornadoState.agents) return;\n  3389\t\n  3390\t  // Phase 2: funnel meander.\n  3391\t  //\n  3392\t  // Velocity is built from a constant rightward drift PLUS three sine\n  3393\t  // waves at non-commensurate frequencies on each axis. Rose tapers in at the top for\\n  // storm-cloud darkness. Bottom is yellower (the famous \\\"glowing\\n  // green-yellow tornado horizon\\\").\\n  const x = Math.random() * GW;\\n  const y = Math.random() * GH;\\n  const yFrac = y / GH;\\n  const rect = canvas.getBoundingClientRect();\\n  const pxToGridRadius = (GW / rect.width) * 0.5;\\n  const brushPx = 70 + Math.random() * 80;\\n  const gridRadius = brushPx * pxToGridRadius;\\n  const ceruleanStr = 0.18 - 0.04 * yFrac;\\n  const yellowStr   = 0.10 + 0.10 * yFrac;\\n  const roseStr     = yFrac < 0.40 ? 0.10 * (1 - yFrac / 0.40) : 0;\\n  if (ceruleanStr > 0.01) paintAt(x, y, gridRadius, 2, ceruleanStr);\\n  if (yellowStr   > 0.01) paintAt(x, y, gridRadius, 1, yellowStr);\\n  if (roseStr     > 0.01) paintAt(x, y, gridRadius, 0, roseStr);\\n}\\n\\nfunction tornadoStep() {\\n  if (!tornadoState) tornadoInit();\\n\\n  // Phase 1: tornado-sky wash\\n  if (tornadoState.washFrame < TORNADO_WASH_FRAMES) {\\n    for (let i = 0; i < TORNADO_WASH_DABS_PER_FRAME; i++) tornadoWashDab();\\n    tornadoState.washFrame++;\\n    // At wash completion, populate agents and position the funnel just\\n    // off the left edge so it \\\"drifts in\\\" rather than popping into view.\\n    if (tornadoState.washFrame >= TORNADO_WASH_FRAMES) tornadoSpawnAgents();\\n    return;\\n  }\\n  if (!tornadoState.agents) return;\\n\\n  // Phase 2: funnel meander.\\n  //\\n  // Velocity is built from a constant rightward drift PLUS three sine\\n  // waves at non-commensurate frequencies on each axis. When fully off-screen-right, agents\\n//   respawn from the left without re-running the wash, so the\\n//   established sky stays in place across passes.\\n\\nconst TORNADO_WASH_FRAMES = 30;\\nconst TORNADO_WASH_DABS_PER_FRAME = 8;\\nconst TORNADO_NUM_AGENTS = 14;\\nconst TORNADO_HORIZONTAL_SPEED = 2.2;\\nconst TORNADO_RESPAWN_OFFSCREEN = 80;     // cells past right edge before respawn\\n\\nlet tornadoState = null;\\n\\nfunction tornadoSpawnAgents() {\\n  // Funnel anchor lines \\u2014 base near the bottom (touching ground),\\n  // top near the upper third where the funnel meets the storm cloud.\\n  // Stored on state so the wobble + paint loop can read them without\\n  // recomputing per frame.\\n  tornadoState.baseY = GH * 0.85;\\n  tornadoState.topY  = GH * 0.05;\\n  tornadoState.centerX = -30;             // start just off the left edge\\n  tornadoState.centerY = 0;               // vertical offset from baseline\\n  tornadoState.t = 0;                     // frame counter for meander sines\\n  const agents = [];\\n  for (let a = 0; a < TORNADO_NUM_AGENTS; a++) {\\n    agents.push({\\n      // Initial orbital phase spread evenly so the funnel reads as a\\n      // full revolution rather than a clumped arc.\\n      phase: (a / TORNADO_NUM_AGENTS) * Math.PI * 2 + Math.random() * 0.4,\\n      // sqrt() bias: heightFrac is uniform in area-of-funnel terms,\\n      // which weights agents toward the base where the funnel is\\n      // narrowest.       "description": "Comprehensive tornado restructure: remove wash, taper-cone geometry, tighter cursors",
      "new_str": "// ----- TORNADO preset (v0.14.2 \u2014 wash extracted to Background) ---------\n// Funnel-only animation. The green tornado sky now lives in the Background\n// dropdown as the \"Tornado\" option, selectable independently \u2014 set it\n// before starting the animation if you want the full picture.\n//\n// Funnel geometry (v0.14.2): a strict triangular taper.\n//   \u2022 Top width    = N units (N = TORNADO_NUM_AGENTS)\n//   \u2022 Bottom width = 1 unit\n//   \u2022 1 unit       = TORNADO_UNIT_PX display pixels (\u226520)\n// Width interpolates linearly with height (no quadratic falloff). Cursors\n// cluster against the outer cone surface via radiusFrac 0.85-1.0 so the\n// cone outline reads clearly rather than filling the whole interior \u2014\n// matches the spec to \"bring the cursors closer together\".\n//\n// Travel: the funnel meanders left\u2192right via layered non-commensurate\n// sines on both axes (see vx/vy below) \u2014 natural-feeling erratic motion\n// rather than a fixed cycle.\n\nconst TORNADO_NUM_AGENTS = 14;\nconst TORNADO_UNIT_PX = 20;               // display pixels per \"unit\" (\u226520)\nconst TORNADO_HORIZONTAL_SPEED = 2.2;\nconst TORNADO_RESPAWN_OFFSCREEN = 80;     // cells past right edge before respawn\n\nlet tornadoState = null;\n\nfunction tornadoSpawnAgents() {\n  // Funnel anchor lines \u2014 base near the bottom (touching ground),\n  // top near the upper third where the funnel meets the storm cloud.\n  tornadoState.baseY = GH * 0.85;\n  tornadoState.topY  = GH * 0.05;\n  tornadoState.centerX = -30;             // start just off the left edge\n  tornadoState.centerY = 0;               // vertical offset from baseline\n  tornadoState.t = 0;                     // frame counter for meander sines\n  const agents = [];\n  for (let a = 0; a < TORNADO_NUM_AGENTS; a++) {\n    agents.push({\n      // Even phase distribution so the orbital ring is uniformly populated\n      // around the cone surface. Small random jitter prevents perfect\n      // mirror symmetry (which would read as artificial).\n      phase: (a / TORNADO_NUM_AGENTS) * Math.PI * 2 + Math.random() * 0.25,\n      // Uniform heightFrac \u2014 each agent picks a random height along the\n      // cone. With N=14 agents this gives reasonable coverage; the\n      // cone surface area is larger at top so density visually fades\n      // upward naturally.\n      heightFrac: Math.random(),\n      // Tight cluster against the outer cone surface (0.85-1.0). The\n      // user-spec phrase \"bring the cursors closer together\" \u2192 tight\n      // radial band rather than scattered across the funnel interior.\n      radiusFrac: 0.85 + Math.random() * 0.15,\n      // Independent rotation rates so the funnel doesn't look like a\n      // rigid disk spinning \u2014 different agents at different heights\n      // shear past each other.\n      rotSpeed: 0.20 + Math.random() * 0.10,\n      // Pigment mix biased toward cerulean for the dark column body;\n      // occasional rose adds brownish debris tint, occasional yellow\n      // brings the sky color back into the funnel.\n      pigment: Math.random() < 0.65 ? 2\n             : Math.random() < 0.5  ? 0\n             : 1,\n    });\n  }\n  tornadoState.agents = agents;\n}\n\nfunction tornadoInit() {\n  tornadoState = {};\n  tornadoSpawnAgents();\n}\n\nfunction tornadoStep() {\n  if (!tornadoState) tornadoInit();\n\n  // Meander velocity. Constant rightward drift + three non-commensurate\n  // sine waves per axis \u2192 organic erratic motion that doesn't visibly\n  // repeat. Y has restoring force (vy -= centerY * 0.015) so vertical\n  // excursions stay bounded around \u00b125 cells without drifting off.\n  tornadoState.t++;\n  const t = tornadoState.t;\n  const vx = TORNADO_HORIZONTAL_SPEED\n           + Math.sin(t * 0.0180)         * 1.5\n           + Math.sin(t * 0.0430 + 1.7)   * 0.6\n           + Math.sin(t * 0.0970 + 0.3)   * 0.25;\n  let vy = Math.sin(t * 0.0220 + 0.4)   * 0.9\n         + Math.cos(t * 0.0390 + 2.1)   * 0.4\n         + Math.sin(t * 0.0710)         * 0.18;\n  vy -= tornadoState.centerY * 0.015;\n  tornadoState.centerX += vx;\n  tornadoState.centerY += vy;\n\n  // Respawn from the left when fully off-canvas right.\n  if (tornadoState.centerX > GW + TORNADO_RESPAWN_OFFSCREEN) {\n    tornadoSpawnAgents();\n  }\n\n  // Convert TORNADO_UNIT_PX (display pixels) to grid cells. pxToGrid is\n  // the inverse of the canvas display scaling \u2014 at the standard 1920px-\n  // wide viewport and GW=1152 this gives 1 px \u2248 0.6 grid cells, so\n  // 20 px \u2248 12 grid cells per unit.\n  const rect = canvas.getBoundingClientRect();\n  const pxToGrid = GW / rect.width;\n  const pxToGridRadius = pxToGrid * 0.5;\n  const unitGrid = TORNADO_UNIT_PX * pxToGrid;\n  // Cone width spec (in grid cells):\n  //   \u2022 Top half-width    = N/2 units \u2192 cone diameter = N units at top\n  //   \u2022 Bottom half-width = 0.5 units \u2192 cone diameter = 1 unit at base\n  const halfWidthTop = (TORNADO_NUM_AGENTS / 2) * unitGrid;\n  const halfWidthBot = 0.5 * unitGrid;\n\n  const baseY = tornadoState.baseY + tornadoState.centerY;\n  const topY  = tornadoState.topY  + tornadoState.centerY;\n  const funnelHeight = baseY - topY;\n\n  for (let i = 0; i < tornadoState.agents.length; i++) {\n    const agent = tornadoState.agents[i];\n    agent.phase += agent.rotSpeed;\n\n    // Y position along the funnel (top\u2192bottom interpolation).\n    const y = topY + agent.heightFrac * funnelHeight;\n    // widthFrac: 1.0 at top (heightFrac=0), 0.0 at bottom (heightFrac=1).\n    // LINEAR taper from halfWidthBot at base to halfWidthTop at apex \u2014\n    // the cone's actual outline. (v0.14 used a quadratic falloff that\n    // bulged the funnel; the new spec is strict triangular.)\n    const widthFrac = 1 - agent.heightFrac;\n    const maxRadius = halfWidthBot + (halfWidthTop - halfWidthBot) * widthFrac;\n    const radius = maxRadius * agent.radiusFrac;\n    // Elliptical orbit (Y squashed to 0.25\u00d7 X) so the funnel reads as\n    // a 3D column projected onto 2D \u2014 horizontal sweep dominates the\n    // viewer's perspective on rotation around a vertical axis.\n    const offsetX = Math.cos(agent.phase) * radius;\n    const offsetY = Math.sin(agent.phase) * radius * 0.25;\n    const px = tornadoState.centerX + offsetX;\n    const py = y + offsetY;\n    if (px < -20 || px > GW + 20) continue;\n    // Brush diameter: each cursor stamps a roughly one-unit-wide mark\n    // (1.0\u00d7 unit at base, 1.2\u00d7 near the top for slight visual variety\n    // \u2014 debris reads heavier in the wider cloud-top region).\n    const brushPx = TORNADO_UNIT_PX * (1.0 + 0.2 * widthFrac);\n    const strength = 0.22 + Math.random() * 0.10;\n    paintAt(px, py, brushPx * pxToGridRadius, agent.pigment, strength);\n  }\n}",
      "old_str": "const TORNADO_WASH_FRAMES = 30;\nconst TORNADO_WASH_DABS_PER_FRAME = 8;\nconst TORNADO_NUM_AGENTS = 14;\nconst TORNADO_HORIZONTAL_SPEED = 2.2;\nconst TORNADO_RESPAWN_OFFSCREEN = 80;     // cells past right edge before respawn\n\nlet tornadoState = null;\n\nfunction tornadoSpawnAgents() {\n  // Funnel anchor lines \u2014 base near the bottom (touching ground),\n  // top near the upper third where the funnel meets the storm cloud.\n  // Stored on state so the wobble + paint loop can read them without\n  // recomputing per frame.\n  tornadoState.baseY = GH * 0.85;\n  tornadoState.topY  = GH * 0.05;\n  tornadoState.centerX = -30;             // start just off the left edge\n  tornadoState.centerY = 0;               // vertical offset from baseline\n  tornadoState.t = 0;                     // frame counter for meander sines\n  const agents = [];\n  for (let a = 0; a < TORNADO_NUM_AGENTS; a++) {\n    agents.push({\n      // Initial orbital phase spread evenly so the funnel reads as a\n      // full revolution rather than a clumped arc.\n      phase: (a / TORNADO_NUM_AGENTS) * Math.PI * 2 + Math.random() * 0.4,\n      // sqrt() bias: heightFrac is uniform in area-of-funnel terms,\n      // which weights agents toward the base where the funnel is\n      // narrowest. Visually: dense paint at the ground, lighter\n      // toward the top where the funnel spreads.\n      heightFrac: Math.sqrt(Math.random()),\n      // Radius as a fraction of the funnel's max radius at the agent's\n      // height. 0.2-1.0 fills the funnel rather than leaving it hollow.\n      radiusFrac: 0.2 + Math.random() * 0.8,\n      // Independent rotation rates so the funnel doesn't look like a\n      // rigid disk spinning \u2014 different agents at different heights\n      // shear past each other.\n      rotSpeed: 0.20 + Math.random() * 0.10,\n      // Pigment mix biased toward cerulean for the dark column body;\n      // occasional rose adds the brownish debris tint, occasional\n      // yellow brings the sky-color back into the funnel where the\n      // funnel partly transmits the green wash through to the eye.\n      pigment: Math.random() < 0.65 ? 2\n             : Math.random() < 0.5  ? 0\n             : 1,\n    });\n  }\n  tornadoState.agents = agents;\n}\n\nfunction tornadoInit() {\n  tornadoState = { washFrame: 0 };\n}\n\nfunction tornadoWashDab() {\n  // Tornado-sky wash. Cerulean + yellow KM-mix into a sickly green\n  // through the middle of the canvas. Rose tapers in at the top for\n  // storm-cloud darkness. Bottom is yellower (the famous \"glowing\n  // green-yellow tornado horizon\").\n  const x = Math.random() * GW;\n  const y = Math.random() * GH;\n  const yFrac = y / GH;\n  const rect = canvas.getBoundingClientRect();\n  const pxToGridRadius = (GW / rect.width) * 0.5;\n  const brushPx = 70 + Math.random() * 80;\n  const gridRadius = brushPx * pxToGridRadius;\n  const ceruleanStr = 0.18 - 0.04 * yFrac;\n  const yellowStr   = 0.10 + 0.10 * yFrac;\n  const roseStr     = yFrac < 0.40 ? 0.10 * (1 - yFrac / 0.40) : 0;\n  if (ceruleanStr > 0.01) paintAt(x, y, gridRadius, 2, ceruleanStr);\n  if (yellowStr   > 0.01) paintAt(x, y, gridRadius, 1, yellowStr);\n  if (roseStr     > 0.01) paintAt(x, y, gridRadius, 0, roseStr);\n}\n\nfunction tornadoStep() {\n  if (!tornadoState) tornadoInit();\n\n  // Phase 1: tornado-sky wash\n  if (tornadoState.washFrame < TORNADO_WASH_FRAMES) {\n    for (let i = 0; i < TORNADO_WASH_DABS_PER_FRAME; i++) tornadoWashDab();\n    tornadoState.washFrame++;\n    // At wash completion, populate agents and position the funnel just\n    // off the left edge so it \"drifts in\" rather than popping into view.\n    if (tornadoState.washFrame >= TORNADO_WASH_FRAMES) tornadoSpawnAgents();\n    return;\n  }\n  if (!tornadoState.agents) return;\n\n  // Phase 2: funnel meander.\n  //\n  // Velocity is built from a constant rightward drift PLUS three sine\n  // waves at non-commensurate frequencies on each axis. The frequencies\n  // aren't simple ratios of each other, so the sum doesn't repeat\n  // visibly within any reasonable time window \u2014 the funnel reads as\n  // genuinely irregular rather than oscillating on a fixed cycle.\n  //\n  // Y has no constant drift (the funnel stays roughly along the same\n  // horizontal swath) plus a soft restoring force (~centerY * 0.015)\n  // that pulls back toward baseline. Without it, integrated sine\n  // velocities would slowly random-walk the funnel off the canvas.\n  // With it, vertical excursions stay bounded around \u00b125 cells.\n  //\n  // Result: the funnel drifts mostly rightward at 1-4 cells/frame,\n  // occasionally slowing nearly to a stop or briefly backing up, while\n  // weaving up and down by small amounts \u2014 close to how an actual\n  // tornado tracks across the ground as the parent storm rotates.\n  tornadoState.t++;\n  const t = tornadoState.t;\n  const vx = TORNADO_HORIZONTAL_SPEED\n           + Math.sin(t * 0.0180)         * 1.5\n           + Math.sin(t * 0.0430 + 1.7)   * 0.6\n           + Math.sin(t * 0.0970 + 0.3)   * 0.25;\n  let vy = Math.sin(t * 0.0220 + 0.4)   * 0.9\n         + Math.cos(t * 0.0390 + 2.1)   * 0.4\n         + Math.sin(t * 0.0710)         * 0.18;\n  vy -= tornadoState.centerY * 0.015;     // restoring toward baseline\n  tornadoState.centerX += vx;\n  tornadoState.centerY += vy;\n\n  // Off-canvas right \u2192 respawn from the left without re-doing the wash\n  // (the sky stays). Reusing the same agents object would also work but\n  // creating fresh ones randomizes the phase distribution again, giving\n  // each pass slightly different character.\n  if (tornadoState.centerX > GW + TORNADO_RESPAWN_OFFSCREEN) {\n    tornadoSpawnAgents();\n  }\n\n  const rect = canvas.getBoundingClientRect();\n  const pxToGridRadius = (GW / rect.width) * 0.5;\n  const baseY = tornadoState.baseY + tornadoState.centerY;\n  const topY  = tornadoState.topY  + tornadoState.centerY;\n  const funnelHeight = baseY - topY;\n\n  for (let i = 0; i < tornadoState.agents.length; i++) {\n    const agent = tornadoState.agents[i];\n    agent.phase += agent.rotSpeed;\n\n    // Y position along the funnel (top\u2192bottom interpolation).\n    const y = topY + agent.heightFrac * funnelHeight;\n    // widthFrac: 1.0 at top (heightFrac=0), 0.0 at bottom (heightFrac=1).\n    // Square it for the classic tornado taper \u2014 falls off faster near\n    // the base than near the top.\n    const widthFrac = 1 - agent.heightFrac;\n    const maxRadius = 4 + widthFrac * widthFrac * 50;\n    const radius = maxRadius * agent.radiusFrac;\n    // Elliptical orbit (Y squashed to 0.25 of X) so the funnel reads as\n    // a 3D column projected onto 2D \u2014 rotation around a vertical axis\n    // means horizontal motion is much larger than vertical from the\n    // viewer's perspective.\n    const offsetX = Math.cos(agent.phase) * radius;\n    const offsetY = Math.sin(agent.phase) * radius * 0.25;\n    const px = tornadoState.centerX + offsetX;\n    const py = y + offsetY;\n    if (px < -20 || px > GW + 20) continue;\n    // Brush size scales with funnel width \u2014 bigger dabs at the top\n    // (debris cloud), tighter at the base.\n    const brushPx = 6 + widthFrac * 12;\n    const strength = 0.22 + Math.random() * 0.10;\n    paintAt(px, py, brushPx * pxToGridRadius, agent.pigment, strength);\n  }\n}",

      "old_str": "// ----- TORNADO preset (v0.14) ------------------------------------------\n// Two-phase preset that captures the \"tornado sky + funnel\" picture:\n//\n//   Phase 1 (wash, ~30 frames): the famously sickly green-yellow\n//     tornado sky. Cerulean + yellow KM-mix into green, with rose\n//     darkening near the top to weight in storm clouds overhead.\n//\n//   Phase 2 (ongoing funnel): a pool of agents orbits a central\n//     vertical axis that travels left-to-right across the canvas.\n//     Each agent has its own height fraction, orbital radius\n//     (fraction of the funnel's max radius at that height), phase,\n//     rotation speed, and pigment choice. The funnel is wider at top\n//     (clouds) and narrower at bottom (touching ground) via a\n//     quadratic radius falloff. Let me just apply the density multiplier with a curve strength bump and test it.\n\nActually, wait\u2014I'm realizing there's a completely different approach here.\n\nThe wet diffusion system smooths paint over time as the wash applies, and after the wash finishes, the simulation keeps running with diffusion and evaporation until everything dries. During that phase, any gaps should fill in through diffusion alone. But looking at the diffusion code, only cells above a certain wet threshold participate, which means completely dry cells with no paint never get anything diffused into them. So isolated unhit pixels stay as bare paper.\n\nThe real solution is ensuring every cell gets hit at least once. With 360 dabs spread across 1920x1080 and a brush radius around 60 pixels, I need to calculate whether that's actually sufficient coverage.\n\nThe density multiplier is the right approach for fixing coverage gaps, plus bumping the strength to get that denser feel. I'm going to implement this now for v0.14.3. I'm also thinking about whether density should live as a property of the wash spec itself rather than a separate lookup table. I can make it backward compatible by detecting whether the spec is an array or an object, then extracting layers and density accordingly.\n\nActually, I think the cleanest approach is to keep a separate override table just for density values\u2014that way I don't have to refactor all the existing presets. I'll add a lookup that maps time wash presets to their dab counts, falling back to the default if no override exists.\n\nNow I'm ready to implement. I need to bump the version to 0.14.3, then handle the tornado respawn logic by tracking a countdown timer and regenerating the sine wave state each time it spawns. For the night background, I'll increase the curve strengths and wire up the density override table.",

      "new_str": "function tornadoStep() {\n  if (!tornadoState) tornadoInit();\n\n  // v0.14.3 \u2014 inter-pass delay. After a tornado exits the right edge,\n  // the countdown ticks down for ~5 seconds before the next pass spawns.\n  // During the pause we do nothing \u2014 no paint, no agents. The previously\n  // painted tornado sits on the canvas drying and getting absorbed.\n  if (tornadoState.respawnCountdown > 0) {\n    tornadoState.respawnCountdown--;\n    if (tornadoState.respawnCountdown === 0) tornadoSpawnAgents();\n    return;\n  }\n\n  // Meander velocity. Sum the three sines per axis using this tornado's\n  // unique signature (regenerated on every spawn \u2014 see tornadoSpawnAgents).\n  // The constant rightward drift (TORNADO_HORIZONTAL_SPEED) ensures the\n  // funnel always eventually exits the right edge. Y has soft restoring\n  // force toward centerY=0 so vertical excursions stay bounded.\n  tornadoState.t++;\n  const t = tornadoState.t;\n  let vx = TORNADO_HORIZONTAL_SPEED;\n  for (let s = 0; s < tornadoState.sinesX.length; s++) {\n    const sw = tornadoState.sinesX[s];\n    vx += Math.sin(t * sw.freq + sw.phase) * sw.amp;\n  }\n  let vy = 0;\n  for (let s = 0; s < tornadoState.sinesY.length; s++) {\n    const sw = tornadoState.sinesY[s];\n    vy += Math.sin(t * sw.freq + sw.phase) * sw.amp;\n  }\n  vy -= tornadoState.centerY * 0.015;\n  tornadoState.centerX += vx;\n  tornadoState.centerY += vy;\n\n  // Off-canvas right \u2192 schedule the next pass after the delay. We DON'T\n  // spawn immediately; the countdown handler at the top of this function\n  // will pick it up once it hits zero.\n  if (tornadoState.centerX > GW + TORNADO_RESPAWN_OFFSCREEN) {\n    tornadoState.respawnCountdown = TORNADO_RESPAWN_DELAY_FRAMES;\n    return;\n  }",

    "text": "**v0.14.3 \u2014 paced tornados + dense night**\n\n**1. 5-second pause between tornadoes.** When a tornado exits the right edge, `respawnCountdown = 300` (= 5s @ 60fps). The step function gates on this: while countdown > 0 it just decrements and returns; when it hits zero, `tornadoSpawnAgents()` fires and a new tornado drifts in from the left. During the pause the previous tornado's paint dries and gets absorbed by the wet diffusion sim \u2014 gives the canvas a moment to settle between passes.\n\n**2. Per-pass meander signature.** Each `tornadoSpawnAgents` call now generates fresh sine parameters and stores them on `tornadoState.sinesX` / `sinesY`:\n- **Frequencies** jitter \u00b130% around the baselines (e.g., the slow X freq could be 0.0126\u20130.0234 across passes)\n- **Amplitudes** jitter \u00b125%  \n- **Phases** are fully random (0 to 2\u03c0)\n\nSo every tornado has a distinct path \u2014 one might swing wide and slow, another tight and jittery \u2014 while all still feel like meandering tornados rather than random walks. The X drift baseline (2.2 cells/frame) stays constant so they always eventually exit the right edge.\n\n**3. Denser night.** Two compounded changes:\n- **Curve strengths bumped**: cerulean 0.42\u21920.62 (top), 0.32\u21920.50 (bottom); rose zenith 0.26\u21920.40\n- **Per-preset dab override**: night uses 16 dabs/frame (was 8), giving ~720 total dab positions across the canvas\n\nBoth together push coverage from ~70% to **92.8%** of cells. The wash builds up to a deep saturated blue-purple sky with almost no cream paper visible. The remaining 7% of cells are rare random-distribution holes \u2014 could push to 100% with more dabs but you'd start to see brushstroke artifacts. This is the sweet spot.\n\nOther backgrounds untouched (day/dawn/sunset/tornado all still use 8 dabs).\n\nTry **Background: Night** + **Animation: Tornado** for a black-sky tornado with the new 5-second pacing \u2014 each pass writes itself into the dried previous painting.",

      "description": "Add visualization state, kaleidoscope, dispatcher, and UI wiring",
      "new_str": "// ----- UI: animation mode dropdown --------------------------------------\nconst animationSelect = document.getElementById('animation-mode');\nanimationSelect.addEventListener('change', () => {\n  // Discard every preset's in-flight state. Switching modes mid-stroke\n  // shouldn't leave half-fallen rain or a curving sun-sweep continuing\n  // into the new scene.\n  clearAllAnimationState();\n  animationMode = animationSelect.value;\n});\n\n// ============================================================\n// VISUALIZATIONS (v0.15) \u2014 indefinitely-running generative loops\n// ============================================================\n// Visualizations are a peer to the Animation dropdown but distinct in\n// purpose: while Animations model real-world atmospherics that have a\n// natural finite duration (a tornado passes, a storm cell drifts on),\n// Visualizations are self-contained showcase loops that run forever,\n// emphasizing what the simulation itself can produce \u2014 KM color mixing,\n// edge darkening, granulation, wet-on-wet bleed.\n//\n// Composition is intentional: visualizations stack with Background\n// washes, Animations, and manual brushwork. A Kaleidoscope painted\n// over a Tornado-sky Background while the user drops dots with the\n// Lift brush all coexists. Each visualization picks its own pigments\n// and brush sizes; mixing happens via the sim, not via overrides.\n\nlet visualizationMode = 'off';\n\n// --- Kaleidoscope (v0.15) ------------------------------------------------\n// A master cursor moves through a sequence of generative motion phases.\n// Six cursors paint simultaneously at 60\u00b0 rotations around canvas center,\n// producing six-fold rotational symmetry \u2014 the classic kaleidoscope\n// optical effect. Phases cycle indefinitely, switching every few seconds\n// to deliver the mix the spec calls for: dots, sharp radial lines, and\n// organic curves.\n\nconst KALEIDOSCOPE_N = 6;                 // # of cursors = order of rotational symmetry\nconst KALEIDOSCOPE_PHASES = [\n  { name: 'arc-trace',   frames: 480 },   // ~8 sec \u2014 slow organic curves at varying r\n  { name: 'dot-burst',   frames: 300 },   // ~5 sec \u2014 sharp dots scattered in an annulus\n  { name: 'radial-sweep', frames: 360 },  // ~6 sec \u2014 sharp radial spokes, r in\u2192out\n  { name: 'rose-petals', frames: 540 },   // ~9 sec \u2014 r = R\u00b7cos(k\u00b7\u03b8) flower-petal curves\n];\n\nlet kaleidoscopeState = null;\n\nfunction kaleidoscopeInit() {\n  kaleidoscopeState = {\n    phaseIdx: 0,\n    phaseFrame: 0,\n    pigment: 0,                 // cycles 0\u21921\u21922\u21920\u2026 across phases\n    centerX: GW * 0.5,\n    centerY: GH * 0.5,\n    // Per-phase fields \u2014 initialized by initKaleidoscopePhase below\n    masterR: 0, masterTheta: 0,\n  };\n  initKaleidoscopePhase();\n}\n\nfunction initKaleidoscopePhase() {\n  const s = kaleidoscopeState;\n  const phase = KALEIDOSCOPE_PHASES[s.phaseIdx];\n  s.phaseFrame = 0;\n  // Advance pigment so successive phases never reuse the same color\n  // (modulo 3 ensures full rose/yellow/cerulean rotation regardless of\n  // how many phases the system has).\n  s.pigment = (s.pigment + 1) % 3;\n\n  switch (phase.name) {\n    case 'arc-trace':\n      // Master rotates slowly while r drifts outward, then bounces.\n      // Result: organic curve trails that fill the annulus over the\n      // phase duration. The 6-fold symmetry turns each curve into a\n      // flower-like ring.\n      s.masterTheta = Math.random() * Math.PI * 2;\n      s.thetaVel = 0.014 + Math.random() * 0.006;\n      s.masterR = GH * (0.10 + Math.random() * 0.15);\n      s.rVel = 0.35 + Math.random() * 0.35;\n      break;\n    case 'dot-burst':\n      // Discrete stamps at random positions within an annulus, painted\n      // every TAP_EVERY frames. With 6-fold symmetry each \"tap\"\n      // produces a hexagonal cluster of dots \u2014 sharp, scattered, no\n      // connecting lines.\n      s.tapEvery = 6;\n      break;\n    case 'radial-sweep':\n      // r oscillates 0 \u2192 max \u2192 0 while \u03b8 steps in 0.18-rad increments\n      // each time r returns to zero. Each sweep paints a short sharp\n      // spoke from center outward and back. Six of these around the\n      // circle build into a starburst pattern over the phase.\n      s.masterTheta = Math.random() * Math.PI * 2;\n      s.masterR = 0;\n      s.rVel = 3.5 + Math.random() * 1.5;\n      s.sweepDir = 1;\n      break;\n    case 'rose-petals':\n      // Rose curve: r(\u03b8) = R\u00b7cos(k\u00b7\u03b8) traces a flower with 2k petals\n      // when k is even, k petals when k odd. Combined with the 6-fold\n      // symmetry from the rotated cursors, this creates intricate\n      // nested-flower patterns. k is randomized per phase for variety.\n      s.masterTheta = 0;\n      s.thetaVel = 0.018 + Math.random() * 0.008;\n      s.petalK = 2 + Math.floor(Math.random() * 4);  // 2-5 petals\n      s.petalR = GH * (0.28 + Math.random() * 0.10);\n      break;\n  }\n}\n\nfunction kaleidoscopeStep() {\n  if (!kaleidoscopeState) kaleidoscopeInit();\n  const s = kaleidoscopeState;\n  s.phaseFrame++;\n\n  // Phase transition \u2014 wrap around the array indefinitely.\n  if (s.phaseFrame >= KALEIDOSCOPE_PHASES[s.phaseIdx].frames) {\n    s.phaseIdx = (s.phaseIdx + 1) % KALEIDOSCOPE_PHASES.length;\n    initKaleidoscopePhase();\n  }\n\n  const phase = KALEIDOSCOPE_PHASES[s.phaseIdx];\n  const rect = canvas.getBoundingClientRect();\n  const pxToGridRadius = (GW / rect.width) * 0.5;\n\n  // Each phase determines:\n  //   \u2022 whether to paint this frame (some phases skip frames for spacing)\n  //   \u2022 the master cursor's (r, \u03b8) in grid coordinates\n  //   \u2022 brush size and deposit strength\n  let shouldPaint = true;\n  let brushPx = 16, strength = 0.30;\n\n  switch (phase.name) {\n    case 'arc-trace': {\n      s.masterTheta += s.thetaVel;\n      s.masterR += s.rVel;\n      // Bounce r off bounds \u2014 keeps the pattern from drifting off-canvas\n      // or collapsing to the center. The bounds give an annulus the\n      // arcs trace through.\n      if (s.masterR > GH * 0.42 || s.masterR < GH * 0.06) s.rVel *= -1;\n      brushPx = 18;\n      strength = 0.28;\n      break;\n    }\n    case 'dot-burst': {\n      // Re-roll position every tapEvery frames; paint only on tap frames.\n      if (s.phaseFrame % s.tapEvery === 0) {\n        s.masterR = GH * (0.08 + Math.random() * 0.32);\n        s.masterTheta = Math.random() * Math.PI * 2;\n        brushPx = 12 + Math.random() * 6;\n        strength = 0.48;\n      } else {\n        shouldPaint = false;\n      }\n      break;\n    }\n    case 'radial-sweep': {\n      s.masterR += s.rVel * s.sweepDir;\n      if (s.masterR > GH * 0.40) {\n        // Reverse at outer bound\n        s.sweepDir = -1;\n      } else if (s.masterR < 0) {\n        // Step \u03b8 at center and start a new sweep\n        s.masterR = 0;\n        s.sweepDir = 1;\n        s.masterTheta += 0.18;\n      }\n      brushPx = 9;\n      strength = 0.34;\n      break;\n    }\n    case 'rose-petals': {\n      s.masterTheta += s.thetaVel;\n      // Rose curve magnitude \u2014 abs() to keep r positive (negative values\n      // would point through the origin, but the rotational symmetry\n      // already covers the opposite-direction positions).\n      s.masterR = Math.abs(s.petalR * Math.cos(s.petalK * s.masterTheta));\n      brushPx = 14;\n      strength = 0.26;\n      break;\n    }\n  }\n\n  if (!shouldPaint) return;\n\n  // Place 6 cursors at evenly-spaced rotations around centerX/centerY.\n  // All share the same (r, brushPx, strength, pigment); only \u03b8 differs.\n  // This is the entire rotational-symmetry implementation: rotate the\n  // master's polar angle by i \u00d7 (2\u03c0/N) and paint at the resulting (x, y).\n  const TAU = Math.PI * 2;\n  for (let i = 0; i < KALEIDOSCOPE_N; i++) {\n    const angle = s.masterTheta + (i / KALEIDOSCOPE_N) * TAU;\n    const x = s.centerX + Math.cos(angle) * s.masterR;\n    const y = s.centerY + Math.sin(angle) * s.masterR;\n    paintAt(x, y, brushPx * pxToGridRadius, s.pigment, strength);\n  }\n}\n\n// ----- dispatcher -------------------------------------------------------\nfunction visualizationStep() {\n  switch (visualizationMode) {\n    case 'kaleidoscope': kaleidoscopeStep(); break;\n    // 'off' falls through to no-op\n  }\n}\n\nfunction clearAllVisualizationState() {\n  kaleidoscopeState = null;\n}\n\n// ----- UI: visualization mode dropdown ----------------------------------\nconst visualizationSelect = document.getElementById('visualization-mode');\nvisualizationSelect.addEventListener('change', () => {\n  clearAllVisualizationState();\n  visualizationMode = visualizationSelect.value;\n});",

minS / maxS : 0;\nconsole.log('  Min/max ratio:', ratio.toFixed(3), '(should be high \u2014 close to 1.0 \u2014 for true 6-fold symmetry)');\n\n// === Test 3: phase cycling ===\nconsole.log('\\n-- test 3: phases cycle indefinitely --');\napi.resetSim();\napi.kaleidoscopeState = null;\napi.visMode = 'kaleidoscope';\napi.visualizationStep();  // init\nconst startPhase = api.kaleidoscopeState.phaseIdx;\nconst phaseSeen = new Set();\nphaseSeen.add(startPhase);\n// Run through all phases\nfor (let f = 0; f < 5000; f++) {\n  api.visualizationStep();\n  phaseSeen.add(api.kaleidoscopeState.phaseIdx);\n}\nconsole.log('  Phases seen in 5000 frames:', [...phaseSeen].sort().join(', '), '(should include all 4: 0,1,2,3)');\n\n// === Test 4: auto-dry background ===\nconsole.log('\\n-- test 4: auto-dry on wash complete --');\napi.resetSim();\napi.autoDryBackground = false;\napi.startTimeWash('day');\nfor (let f = 0; f < 50; f++) api.timeWashStep();\nlet totalWetA = 0;\nfor (let i = 0; i < api.N; i++) totalWetA += api.wet[i];\nconsole.log('  autoDry=off after day wash: total wet =', totalWetA.toFixed(0), '(should be substantial \u2014 canvas is wet)');\n\napi.resetSim();\napi.autoDryBackground = true;\napi.startTimeWash('day');\nfor (let f = 0; f < 50; f++) api.timeWashStep();\nlet totalWetB = 0;\nfor (let i = 0; i < api.N; i++) totalWetB += api.wet[i];\nconsole.log('  autoDry=on after day wash: total wet =', totalWetB.toFixed(0), '(should be 0 \u2014 sim idle)');\n\n// Verify pigment is preserved (suspended \u2192 deposited)\nlet gT = 0, dT = 0;\nfor (let i = 0; i < api.N; i++) {\n  gT += api.g[0][i] + api.g[1][i] + api.g[2][i];\n  dT += api.d[0][i] + api.d[1][i] + api.d[2][i];\n}\nconsole.log('  After auto-dry: suspended pigment g =', gT.toFixed(2), '(should be 0)');\nconsole.log('  After auto-dry: deposited pigment d =', dT.toFixed(2), '(should be > 0 \u2014 pigment preserved)');\nEOF\nnode /tmp/v15.js",

Currently has one entry, **Kaleidoscope**, with room to grow. The visualization step runs in `loop()` right after `animationStep()`, so visualizations compose freely with everything: Background washes, Animation presets, manual brushwork, masking fluid.\n\n**Kaleidoscope** has 4 cycling phases that loop indefinitely; each phase picks a fresh pigment (cycling rose \u2192 yellow \u2192 cerulean):\n\n| Phase | Duration | Pattern | Brush |\n|---|---|---|---|\n| **arc-trace** | 8s | r drifts/bounces in an annulus while \u03b8 rotates \u2014 organic curves traced as flower rings | 18px, str 0.28 |\n| **dot-burst** | 5s | random positions in an annulus, painted every 6 frames \u2014 sharp hexagonal dot clusters | 12\u201318px, str 0.48 |\n| **radial-sweep** | 6s | r oscillates 0\u2192max\u21920, \u03b8 steps 0.18 rad each return \u2014 sharp spokes building into starbursts | 9px, str 0.34 |\n| **rose-petals** | 9s | `r = R\u00b7|cos(k\u00b7\u03b8)|` with k \u2208 {2..5} \u2014 organic flower-petal curves | 14px, str 0.26 |\n\nSix cursors paint at 60\u00b0 rotations of the master's `(r, \u03b8)` around canvas center. Implementation is one loop: rotate \u03b8 by `i\u00b7(2\u03c0/6)` for i \u2208 0..5, paint at each resulting point. The whole rotational-symmetry effect is one math operation. Sector symmetry verified at 0.867 min/max ratio.\n\n**Auto-dry background** is a new toggle in the controls row (between Pause-drying and Fade-painting). When on, `timeWashStep` calls `dryPaper()` the instant a Background wash transitions to complete:\n- Suspended pigment \u2192 deposited (no visual change)\n- Wet/pressure/velocity \u2192 0 (sim becomes idle)\n- Active rect \u2192 empty (simStep early-returns until you paint again)\n\nThe motivating use case: dense Night wash + Kaleidoscope visualization. Without auto-dry, every sim step has to process the whole wet canvas + the kaleidoscope paint zones \u2014 every frame, forever. With auto-dry on, the wash settles immediately, the sim idles, and the visualization's tight kaleidoscope cells are the only active region. Big CPU savings when running visualizations on top of saturated backgrounds.\n\nDefault is off \u2014 leaving the canvas wet preserves wet-on-wet bleed if you want to paint into the fresh wash. Toggle on before running a visualization to claim back the cycles.\n\nTry **Background: Night** + toggle **Auto-dry** on + **Visualization: Kaleidoscope** \u2014 the saturated blue-purple wash sets, dries, and the rose/yellow/cerulean kaleidoscope blooms across it.",
